LEAF-G157-001.title: Re-read frozen authorities, corrective successor evidence, and coefficient-control surfaces
LEAF-G157-001.objective: Start from repo truth only before activating a new pack.
LEAF-G157-001.workflow_intent_assertion: Planning bootstrap only; no runtime semantic change is allowed.
ordered_actions: Reread the frozen law surfaces and the current router before creating a new pack.
Trace the current governed coefficient authority, salvage/example registry, workbook evidence, and runtime consumer files so the new pack starts from observed repo truth.
Confirm that the new work category is coefficient architecture consolidation for Workstreams 1-4 rather than a continuation of the Gate 150-156 seam-hardening category.
Confirm that the independent risk lane remains separate successor work rather than an in-scope bootstrap subject.
forbidden_actions: Do not create a new pack merely because the old one is nearby.
Do not start writing gates before re-reading the active vocabulary and packet authorities.
Do not assume workbook tabs are runtime authority because they are rich.
Do not silently absorb Workstream 5 risk-lane scope into this pack.
expected_evidence: bootstrap rationale is grounded in repo files and workbook evidence already present in the repo
pack category change is explicit before any new gate text is written
definition_of_done: the bootstrap rationale can point to observed repo artefacts only
the new pack boundary against the independent risk lane is explicit
LEAF-G157-001.packaging_requirement: Fresh full-history zip required at operator handoff after the gate is green.

LEAF-G157-002.title: Write the active planning quartet, scope note, and Gate 157 receipt
LEAF-G157-002.objective: Populate the new pack from repo truth only.
LEAF-G157-002.workflow_intent_assertion: Creates the new active planning scaffolding and bounded-scope note that later execution must obey literally.
ordered_actions: Write one active gates master for Gates 157-163 with a final audit gate and no placeholder rows.
Write one leaves ledger with detailed ordered actions, forbidden actions, validations, expected evidence, and definitions of done for every future leaf.
Write one execution log and one document-touch checklist that match the new pack exactly.
Write one bounded-scope note that freezes the approved Workstreams 1-4 brief, workbook gold, and out-of-scope boundary.
Write a Gate 157 receipt that records why this pack now exists and why Gate 156 remains historical evidence.
forbidden_actions: Do not leave placeholder sections or empty future leaves.
Do not omit the final audit / closeout gate.
Do not treat descriptive phrases as governed vocabulary by stealth.
Do not rely on chat memory instead of repo-native scope documentation.
expected_evidence: new active pack exists with bounded Gates 157-163
scope note captures workbook gold and Workstream 1-4 boundary
future gate leaves are materially detailed
definition_of_done: the new active pack is internally coherent
the scope note is enough for a later coding thread to understand what this pack is and is not
LEAF-G157-002.packaging_requirement: Fresh full-history zip required at operator handoff after the gate is green.

LEAF-G157-003.title: Route the repo truthfully to Gate 158 and harden planning guards
LEAF-G157-003.objective: Update router surfaces and planning tests so the new pack is the truthful active authority.
LEAF-G157-003.workflow_intent_assertion: Router-only and planning-guard work; runtime behaviour must remain at the Gate 156 baseline.
ordered_actions: Move `PLANS.md` from the no-active-pack state to the new coefficient architecture consolidation pack.
Update the canonical gate map so Gate 157 is complete and Gate 158 is the active gate on the work branch.
Append a changelog entry for the planning activation.
Add or amend planning tests only where router truth or active-pack truth has changed.
forbidden_actions: Do not leave `PLANS.md`, the gate map, the leaves ledger, and the execution log disagreeing about the active gate.
Do not claim a merged-`main` state that has not happened.
Do not change runtime code while updating router truth.
Do not omit the bounded-scope note from the active router if the pack relies on it.
expected_evidence: router, gate map, leaves ledger, execution log, and scope note all point to Gate 158 as the next active gate
planning guard test covers the new active pack state
definition_of_done: repo control surfaces agree on the new active pack
the planning guard test passes on the declared proof slice
LEAF-G157-003.packaging_requirement: Fresh full-history zip required at operator handoff after the gate is green.

LEAF-G157-004.title: Package the exact work-branch state for operator handoff
LEAF-G157-004.objective: Create the durable artefact that protects the planning work from sandbox loss.
LEAF-G157-004.workflow_intent_assertion: Packaging-only leaf; no semantic repo change is allowed beyond the artefact itself.
ordered_actions: Package the exact repo state from the work branch as a fresh full-history zip.
Exclude only `.venv` and cache clutter.
Record the packaging artefact in the operator reply so the state can be rehydrated later.
forbidden_actions: Do not omit `.git/` from the handoff artefact.
Do not package a stale repo state different from the one just validated.
Do not claim a merged-`main` state that does not exist.
expected_evidence: fresh full-history zip for the exact work-branch state exists
definition_of_done: the branch state can be rehydrated without losing the planning work
LEAF-G157-004.packaging_requirement: Fresh full-history zip required at operator handoff after the gate is green.

LEAF-G158-001.title: Consolidate repo-native coefficient architecture vocabulary and family law
LEAF-G158-001.objective: Write one repo-native architecture receipt for Workstream 1 using existing terms first.
LEAF-G158-001.workflow_intent_assertion: Planning-only; architecture consolidation without runtime code edits.
ordered_actions: Map the repo's existing architecture terms to the coefficient-family architecture without inventing new vocabulary unless required.
State exactly which coefficient-family concepts already exist in doctrine, workbook, and runtime surfaces.
Freeze which descriptive phrases remain descriptive only until later vocabulary admission.
Record the retain / descriptive-only / candidate-new-term split explicitly in the receipt.
forbidden_actions: Do not rewrite repo history as if the architecture were absent.
Do not admit new governed vocabulary without updating the vocabulary authority and builder together.
Do not flatten distinct concepts merely because they sound adjacent.
expected_evidence: one explicit repo-native coefficient architecture statement exists
vocabulary sufficiency or insufficiency is recorded with proof
definition_of_done: later coding threads no longer need chat memory to understand the target architecture
any vocabulary additions are either admitted properly or explicitly rejected
LEAF-G158-001.packaging_requirement: Fresh full-history zip required at operator handoff after the gate is green.

LEAF-G158-002.title: Freeze workbook raw-versus-derived and Step 1 stage-purity law as pack constraints
LEAF-G158-002.objective: Promote the workbook's raw-first and stage-purity rules into explicit later-gate constraints.
LEAF-G158-002.workflow_intent_assertion: Planning-only; this leaf constrains future work rather than changing runtime behaviour.
inputs: workbook sheets README, Raw_Primitives_Catalog, Derived_Features_Catalog, Temporal_Step1_Framework
ordered_actions: Extract the raw-versus-derived ordering law from the workbook and state it in repo planning form.
Extract the Step 1 stage-purity prohibitions and state what later gates must not violate.
Tie those workbook rules to the repo's current stage grammar so future coefficient work cannot use downstream derived outputs as Step 1 crutches.
forbidden_actions: Do not treat workbook heuristics as runtime truth just because they are useful.
Do not allow later coefficient work to blur missing raw-data design into fake dynamic cleverness.
Do not weaken stage purity into vague advice.
expected_evidence: receipt carries explicit raw-first / derived-second and Step 1 stage-purity constraints
definition_of_done: later gates are bound by explicit workbook-derived constraints, not vague reminders
LEAF-G158-002.packaging_requirement: Fresh full-history zip required at operator handoff after the gate is green.

LEAF-G158-003.title: Decide whether any new governed vocabulary is actually required
LEAF-G158-003.objective: Force a yes-or-no decision on vocabulary admission instead of letting descriptive phrases drift.
LEAF-G158-003.workflow_intent_assertion: Vocabulary-governance leaf only; runtime behaviour must not change here.
inputs: candidate descriptive phrases from the scope note
ordered_actions: Check whether existing canonical terms can already express coefficient-world status, activation-state truth, and opportunity-versus-caution boundaries.
If existing terms suffice, record a no-new-terms verdict explicitly.
If existing terms do not suffice, admit the minimum new governed vocabulary and update the builder in the same gate.
forbidden_actions: Do not add vocabulary for convenience or aesthetics.
Do not leave ambiguous descriptive phrases ungoverned if the receipt proves they are required repeatedly.
Do not change vocabulary without updating the builder.
expected_evidence: receipt contains an explicit vocabulary sufficiency decision
vocabulary authority and builder stay synchronized if touched
definition_of_done: the pack either stays on existing terms cleanly or admits the minimum required additions honestly
LEAF-G158-003.packaging_requirement: Fresh full-history zip required at operator handoff after the gate is green.

LEAF-G158-004.title: Advance the pack from Gate 158 to Gate 159 honestly
LEAF-G158-004.objective: Move the router only after the Workstream 1 planning receipt is green.
ordered_actions: Update the planning quartet together so Gate 158 is complete and Gate 159 is active.
Append a changelog entry for the Gate 158 closeout.
Keep the scope note synchronized if the gate changed any pack boundary or vocabulary decision.
forbidden_actions: Do not leave router surfaces disagreeing about the active gate.
Do not call Gate 158 complete until its proof slice is green.
Do not forget to record whether vocabulary changed or not.
expected_evidence: router, gate map, leaves ledger, and execution log all agree that Gate 159 is active
definition_of_done: Gate 158 closes honestly and the pack moves forward without drift
LEAF-G158-004.packaging_requirement: Fresh full-history zip required at operator handoff after the gate is green.

LEAF-G159-001.title: Classify live, reference, provenance, and deferred coefficient worlds explicitly
LEAF-G159-001.objective: Write the classification law for one live coefficient world.
LEAF-G159-001.workflow_intent_assertion: Planning-only; classification law before any config/runtime edit.
ordered_actions: Define the allowed coefficient-world classes for this repo: live runtime authority, reference-only, provenance-only, deferred, and any narrowly justified sub-class.
Map existing config and workbook surfaces into those classes without pretending they are all equivalent.
Record the human-facing question this classification must answer on bad days: what is live and what is not.
forbidden_actions: Do not collapse live and provenance into one vague 'source of truth'.
Do not invent extra status classes without evidence.
Do not treat workbook tabs as runtime config.
expected_evidence: receipt carries an explicit coefficient-world class system
current repo surfaces are mapped into that system
definition_of_done: the repo has an explicit answer to the two-coefficient-world problem
LEAF-G159-001.packaging_requirement: Fresh full-history zip required at operator handoff after the gate is green.

LEAF-G159-002.title: Define the repo-native coefficient-status inventory
LEAF-G159-002.objective: Specify the inventory surface later coding must populate or maintain.
LEAF-G159-002.workflow_intent_assertion: Planning-only; defines the inventory law without claiming it already exists in code.
ordered_actions: Define the minimum fields the coefficient-status inventory must carry, including source class, owner stage, current source of truth, and migration priority.
Tie the inventory law back to the workbook's `Coeff_Universe_Index` and `Runtime_Surface_Drivers` without promoting Excel into runtime truth.
State which inventory answers belong in repo-native artefacts rather than in chat or spreadsheet memory.
forbidden_actions: Do not define an inventory so broad it becomes a second registry without purpose.
Do not skip migration priority or current source-of-truth fields.
Do not leave the workbook-to-repo relationship implicit.
expected_evidence: receipt defines the coefficient-status inventory fields and purpose
definition_of_done: later coding threads know what inventory they are expected to build or maintain
LEAF-G159-002.packaging_requirement: Fresh full-history zip required at operator handoff after the gate is green.

LEAF-G159-003.title: Freeze migration law for workbook and salvage surfaces
LEAF-G159-003.objective: Record how workbook and salvage surfaces may influence repo evolution without becoming live by accident.
ordered_actions: State the allowed migration path from workbook or salvage/reference surface into governed runtime authority.
State the proof burden required before a workbook or salvage surface can be promoted.
State what must remain reference-only or provenance-only even if it is useful context.
forbidden_actions: Do not let later work promote a surface just because it already has min/max columns.
Do not treat salvage/example config as a silent fallback runtime source.
Do not conflate historical provenance with approved live admission.
expected_evidence: receipt defines how non-live coefficient surfaces may and may not be promoted
definition_of_done: promotion path from workbook or salvage material is explicit and bounded
LEAF-G159-003.packaging_requirement: Fresh full-history zip required at operator handoff after the gate is green.

LEAF-G159-004.title: Advance the pack from Gate 159 to Gate 160 honestly
LEAF-G159-004.objective: Close the coefficient-world planning gate without drift.
ordered_actions: Update the planning quartet together so Gate 159 is complete and Gate 160 is active.
Append a changelog entry for the Gate 159 closeout.
Keep the scope note synchronized if the gate changed any classification or migration boundary.
forbidden_actions: Do not leave the router in a half-updated state.
Do not call Gate 159 complete until the declared proof slice is green.
Do not forget to carry forward the explicit promotion law.
expected_evidence: router and execution log agree that Gate 160 is active
definition_of_done: Gate 159 closes honestly and preserves the coefficient-world classification law
LEAF-G159-004.packaging_requirement: Fresh full-history zip required at operator handoff after the gate is green.

LEAF-G160-001.title: Build the owner-stage and activation-state ledger for admitted mutable surfaces
LEAF-G160-001.objective: Create the planning ledger that joins owner labels, actual consumers, and dynamic activation state.
LEAF-G160-001.workflow_intent_assertion: Planning-only; owner/consumer/activation law before runtime edits.
ordered_actions: List every admitted mutable surface from the governed authority file.
For each surface, record the declared owner stage, the actual runtime consumer path, and whether the surface is dynamically active or baseline-only today.
Use existing seam-hardening receipts where relevant instead of re-inventing owner-stage analysis from scratch.
forbidden_actions: Do not talk only at broad stage level when field-level consumer truth is visible.
Do not assume every governed surface is dynamically active.
Do not blur direct consumers and compatibility wrappers.
expected_evidence: ledger exists for all admitted mutable surfaces
activation-state truth is explicit per surface
definition_of_done: owner-stage and activation-state truth are visible in one planning receipt
LEAF-G160-001.packaging_requirement: Fresh full-history zip required at operator handoff after the gate is green.

LEAF-G160-002.title: Freeze mismatch classes and allowed closure modes
LEAF-G160-002.objective: Define how later coding may resolve owner-stage mismatches.
LEAF-G160-002.workflow_intent_assertion: Planning-only; mismatch law before code work.
ordered_actions: Define the allowed closure modes for mismatch cases: rewire consumer, relabel owner stage, defer dynamic use, or preserve bounded compatibility with explicit rationale.
Apply those modes explicitly to the known pressure points such as Stage 5 / Stage 6 and posture / later-risk threshold cases.
State what later coding must prove before any mismatch can be called resolved.
forbidden_actions: Do not hand-wave mismatches as harmless because the system is alpha.
Do not treat comments or prose-only caveats as closure.
Do not hide deferred cases by leaving them ambiguous.
expected_evidence: receipt defines mismatch classes and allowed closure modes
definition_of_done: later coding threads know exactly how a mismatch may be resolved and what counts as proof
LEAF-G160-002.packaging_requirement: Fresh full-history zip required at operator handoff after the gate is green.

LEAF-G160-003.title: Freeze direct-consumer versus compatibility-carriage interpretation
LEAF-G160-003.objective: Separate true live consumers from compatibility-only carriage in the coefficient context.
ordered_actions: Record which surfaces are directly consumed for live decision behaviour versus merely carried for review, compatibility, or later migration.
Tie that distinction back to the activation-state ledger so future work does not overstate current live dynamic behaviour.
State how later coding should document direct-consumer promotion or retirement of compatibility carriage.
forbidden_actions: Do not call a carried field an active behavioural consumer without proof.
Do not let review visibility masquerade as live control authority.
Do not leave compatibility-only carriage unlabeled.
expected_evidence: receipt distinguishes direct consumers from compatibility or review carriage
definition_of_done: activation-state truth is not inflated by simple packet carriage
LEAF-G160-003.packaging_requirement: Fresh full-history zip required at operator handoff after the gate is green.

LEAF-G161-001.title: Freeze the current caution-heavy reality honestly
LEAF-G161-001.objective: State exactly what the current live modifier plane does today before proposing a cleaner split.
LEAF-G161-001.workflow_intent_assertion: Planning-only; truth-reporting on current live behaviour.
inputs: current live mutable surfaces and modifier policies
ordered_actions: Describe the current live modifier plane as it actually behaves today, including the caution-heavy surface set and downstream expression effects.
Record what is currently active in posture, execution, or later risk-sensitive surfaces.
State explicitly what is not yet a true upstream opportunity branch.
forbidden_actions: Do not pretend the current live dynamic scheme is already a balanced opportunity-and-risk architecture.
Do not use flattering language to blur caution-heavy reality.
Do not infer upstream dynamic opportunity shaping where no such consumer path exists.

LEAF-G161-002.title: Freeze the upstream opportunity path through primitives, derived features, and playbook routing
LEAF-G161-002.objective: Use the workbook to define where richer opportunity architecture should come from first.

ordered_actions: State explicitly that richer upstream opportunity shaping should come first from better raw primitives, better derived features, and better playbook-family routing.
Use the workbook evidence to name the relevant input and feature families.
Tie that path back to the repo's existing stage grammar so future work does not solve missing input design by coefficient inflation.

Do not ignore workbook evidence that better inputs and playbook routing already exist as the stronger path.
Do not let Step 1 consume downstream outputs just to create the appearance of opportunity shaping.

LEAF-G161-003.title: Freeze bounded-surface discipline and non-duplication law
LEAF-G161-003.objective: Keep live coefficient surfaces narrow while separating opportunity and caution responsibilities.
ordered_actions: State that the admitted live mutable surface set remains intentionally narrow until owner-stage truth and the opportunity-versus-caution split are closed.
State that caution shaping and later independent risk work must not silently duplicate one another.
Record what this pack can say now without claiming to solve the independent risk lane itself.
forbidden_actions: Do not widen the live mutable surface list during this planning gate.
Do not claim serial conservatism is solved here if the risk lane remains separate future work.
Do not let bounded-surface discipline collapse into vague 'be careful' wording.

definition_of_done: the pack can separate opportunity and caution planning without accidentally widening runtime knobs

LEAF-G162-001.title: Translate Gates 158-161 into one execution-ready coding sequence
LEAF-G162-001.objective: Turn the first four workstreams into an explicit implementation order.
ordered_actions: Convert the outputs of Gates 158-161 into one exact coding order rather than a loose list of ideas.

State which problems must be solved before widening live behaviour and which can remain deferred.
